stem: A food manufacturing study measures the weight (grams) for a sample of 25 cereal boxes. The sample mean is 318.8 g with a sample standard deviation of 83.9 g. However, 2 of the 25 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 23 observations have a mean of 308.6 g and standard deviation of 52.2 g. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 318.8, 95% CI = [284.2, 353.4]. Without outliers: mean = 308.6, 95% CI = [286.8, 330.4]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [284.2, 353.4]. Point estimate: 313.7 g.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [284.2, 353.4].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
